title: plumeria obtusa
intro: Plumeria obtusa, also known as the Pagoda tree or the Kanaka tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Southeast Asian and Pacific Island cultures, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Plumeria obtusa include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, as well as supporting digestive and respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of the Plumeria obtusa plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, and are also used in aromatherapy due to their calming and soothing effects.
